file.content:
-- $Id: testes/closure.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print "testing closures"

local A,B = 0,{g=10}
local function f(x)
  local a = {}
  for i=1,1000 do
    local y = 0
    do
      a[i] = function () B.g = B.g+1; y = y+x; return y+A end
    end
  end
  local dummy = function () return a[A] end
  collectgarbage()
  A = 1; assert(dummy() == a[1]); A = 0;
  assert(a[1]() == x)
  assert(a[3]() == x)
  collectgarbage()
  assert(B.g == 12)
  return a
end

local a = f(10)
-- force a GC in this level
local x = {[1] = {}}   -- to detect a GC
setmetatable(x, {__mode = 'kv'})
while x[1] do   -- repeat until GC
  local a = A..A..A..A  -- create garbage
  A = A+1
end
assert(a[1]() == 20+A)
assert(a[1]() == 30+A)
assert(a[2]() == 10+A)
collectgarbage()
assert(a[2]() == 20+A)
assert(a[2]() == 30+A)
assert(a[3]() == 20+A)
assert(a[8]() == 10+A)
assert(getmetatable(x).__mode == 'kv')
assert(B.g == 19)


-- testing equality
a = {}

for i = 1, 5 do  a[i] = function (x) return i + a + _ENV end  end
assert(a[3] ~= a[4] and a[4] ~= a[5])

delta.text: Build is green (
only a pre-existing warning). Per instructions, I won't re-run the test — the mega-loop will re

-test.

**Root cause:** Three locations in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs` mishandle upvalue closing on return rel
ative to the C reference (`reference/lua-c/lvm.c:1716-

1775`):

1. `OpCode::Return` (vm.rs:2207) called `close
_upvals(base)` unconditionally and *before* the top-adjustment, but C only calls it inside `if (TES

TARG_k(i))` and after setting `L->top.p = ci->top.p`.
2. `OpC
ode::Return0` (vm.rs:2242) called `close_upvals(base)`, but C never closes here

.
3. `OpCode::Return1` (vm.rs:2264) — same bug; this was

the panic site.

The codegen's `cg_finish` (lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1790) already upgrades
`RETURN0/RETURN1 → RETURN+k` when `needclose` is set, so executing `RETURN0/RETURN1` should
never need a close. With Rust's incorrect closure-close ordering, it tried to close an upvalue at id
x 13 with `top=12`, tripping the invariant `uplevel(

uv) < L->top.p`.

**Fix:** Moved `close_upvals(base)` inside `if i.test_k()` and after `set
_top(ci_top)` in `OP_RETURN`; removed the spurious `close_upvals` call from `OP_RETURN0` and `OP_RETURN1`.

Also removed the diagnostic `eprintln!` from `func.rs`.

**New state:** Build green. Did not re-test (mega-loop will verify

content.text:
Build is green (only a pre-existing warning). Per instructions, I won't re-run the test — the mega-loop will re-test.

**Root cause:** Three locations in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs` mishandle upvalue closing on return relative to the C reference (`reference/lua-c/lvm.c:1716-1775`):

1. `OpCode::Return` (vm.rs:2207) called `close_upvals(base)` unconditionally and *before* the top-adjustment, but C only calls it inside `if (TESTARG_k(i))` and after setting `L->top.p = ci->top.p`.
2. `OpCode::Return0` (vm.rs:2242) called `close_upvals(base)`, but C never closes here.
3. `OpCode::Return1` (vm.rs:2264) — same bug; this was the panic site.

The codegen's `cg_finish` (lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1790) already upgrades `RETURN0/RETURN1 → RETURN+k` when `needclose` is set, so executing `RETURN0/RETURN1` should never need a close. With Rust's incorrect closure-close ordering, it tried to close an upvalue at idx 13 with `top=12`, tripping the invariant `uplevel(uv) < L->top.p`.

**Fix:** Moved `close_upvals(base)` inside `if i.test_k()` and after `set_top(ci_top)` in `OP_RETURN`; removed the spurious `close_upvals` call from `OP_RETURN0` and `OP_RETURN1`. Also removed the diagnostic `eprintln!` from `func.rs`.

**New state:** Build green. Did not re-test (mega-loop will verify).

result:
Build is green (only a pre-existing warning). Per instructions, I won't re-run the test — the mega-loop will re-test.

**Root cause:** Three locations in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs` mishandle upvalue closing on return relative to the C reference (`reference/lua-c/lvm.c:1716-1775`):

1. `OpCode::Return` (vm.rs:2207) called `close_upvals(base)` unconditionally and *before* the top-adjustment, but C only calls it inside `if (TESTARG_k(i))` and after setting `L->top.p = ci->top.p`.
2. `OpCode::Return0` (vm.rs:2242) called `close_upvals(base)`, but C never closes here.
3. `OpCode::Return1` (vm.rs:2264) — same bug; this was the panic site.

The codegen's `cg_finish` (lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1790) already upgrades `RETURN0/RETURN1 → RETURN+k` when `needclose` is set, so executing `RETURN0/RETURN1` should never need a close. With Rust's incorrect closure-close ordering, it tried to close an upvalue at idx 13 with `top=12`, tripping the invariant `uplevel(uv) < L->top.p`.

**Fix:** Moved `close_upvals(base)` inside `if i.test_k()` and after `set_top(ci_top)` in `OP_RETURN`; removed the spurious `close_upvals` call from `OP_RETURN0` and `OP_RETURN1`. Also removed the diagnostic `eprintln!` from `func.rs`.

**New state:** Build green. Did not re-test (mega-loop will verify).
